mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that raises discount rates on future corporate earnings, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 134,966 thousand as of March 2006
failure_condition: Payroll declines do not precede SPX negative returns by 60 days in at least 50% of instances, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in housing starts below 1,800 thousand units signals weakening residential investment demand and reduced construction employment multiplier effects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 2,120 thousand as of February 2006
regime_hint: housing cycle peak with demand deterioration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances when HOUST < 1800, or Spearman rank correlation between HOUST and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index growth rate decelerates below 2% annualized, indicating slowing manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate capital expenditure plans, which reduces equity earnings growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth and housing starts move in opposite directions: when RSAFS growth decelerates while HOUST remains elevated, it signals demand rotation away from goods toward services and housing, creating earnings uncertainty that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 361,046 million and HOUST at 2,120 thousand as of March 2006
regime_hint: demand composition shift in late-cycle expansion
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction effect (measured as coefficient on RSAFS × HOUST interaction term) is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% AND core CPI inflation remains above 2.5%, the Fed is in restrictive tightening mode with limited easing capacity, signaling sustained earnings pressure and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.59% and CPIAUCSL at 199.1 as of March 2006
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances when both conditions are met, or the joint effect is not significantly more negative than either condition alone (interaction p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.8% signals labor market slack expansion that reduces wage pressure and consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as inflation expectations decline and Fed easing becomes more probable.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening enabling monetary easing
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 4.8%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth deceleration combined with rising unemployment signals demand-side weakness that compresses corporate profit margins and equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as recession probability increases.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 2.5% annualized and UNRATE rising > 0.2pp in 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,248.3 billion and UNRATE at 4.7% as of March 2006
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient is not significantly negative (p > 0.05) in walk-forward regression.
